review_text: Well done, we had car trouble leaving port Lincoln need to drop van and go back into town, , it was a couple of hours using power but they didn't charge us.thankyou

I try to avoid caravan parks at the best of times - mainly due to overcrowding and noisy neighbours. Give me simple bush camping any day.
Having said that, if you want to explore Port Lincoln and stay close by then a caravan park is the only option. I initially stayed here one night on a powered site by the water and thought it was great as far as caravan parks go. I came back for another night, followed by 4 nights in-between visiting national parks.

Rochelle who always greets you with a friendly smile and provides excellent tips on places to visit, Sandy who keeps the facilities very clean and Billy who will happily help out if you need a hand with anything. These people all add to guests feeling welcome and appreciated.

The sites are very good and the showers are pretty good too. Yes, the facilities are old but are indeed very clean.
There is some noise from the highway, but I didn't find it intrusive - being an 80 km/hr area would help.
The roadhouse next door provides some great takeaway food.
When I next visit, I wouldn't hesitate to stay here again.
One tip I would like to give - and this is for all caravan parks - to provide separate bins for recycling purposes 😉 (cardboard and plastics that don't have 10ct refund).

A great park with great views. Friendly staff at the office. Large powered sites  with some privacy from trees. Clean amenities & daily servicing, although a bit dated in good order. Conveniently located within easy reach of Pt Lincoln. Servo also located just outside the park with lots to offer. We extended our stay as it was so nice.
Very friendly managers. Huge caravan sites separated by bushy copy olive trees. Amities older style but very clean. Great oceanfront location

Waterfront 10mins from town.
Dated ablutions but awesome showers.
Great park right on the bay. About 10kms from Port lincoln. Nice and quiet
Cabin's quite small, lot's of rooms for caravans, motorhomes tent's
Booked this place last minute, Rochelle the receptionist was so kind and went out of her way to help us even after hours. We got a brilliant spot right on the sea - couldn’t be better.
We really enjoyed our three-night stay. The bathrooms were exceptionally clean, check-in was easy and accessible, and the location was perfect for exploring Port Lincoln and the surrounding area.
Booked campsites. Very accessible location. Great facilities.
A bit out of town but there is a walking track to town.
It's not quite port Lincoln but only ten minutes out. That ten minutes gets you a ocean front park at a reasonable price. Amenities are good.
Very tired facility, clean amenities
Great place to be for the weekend

Paid $ 240 a week for powered site. Amenities were little dated, however very clean and tidy with no toilets or showers out of order to my surprise 😲
Lovely park with water views and courteous helpful staff.👍

A pleasant welcome and a clean cabin with a well equipped kitchenette. The electric frypan came in handy but Im not sure how the saucepan could be used without a stove|Top? The ensuite is tiny but functional
Great location. Friendly staff. Toilets could do with an upgrade but hot powerful shower made up for it.
The girls in the tourist office are extremely helpful. Made great recommendations on what to do and see whilst we were in Port Lincoln. Thank you 😊
Great caravan park on the water. The park is nice and clean and so are the amenities blocks. Highly recommend.

> On the sea - great views.
We visited in April 2026, 1 night in a Cabin.
The huge Cabin was neat, tidy and comfortable!
The Park is very organised, neat and clean.
Service was excellent and warm - Thank you.
(We have stayed an many G'Day Parks on our road-trips).
The view of the sea, the walk along the jetty was great.
And the sunset with the full moon was a good experience.
Port Lincoln cafe's and attractions nearby.
Coffin Bay for Oysters is about 40km away.
We enjoyed our stay there.
Yes, well recommended.
Thank you.
Massive Caravan Park close to town and by the ocean. Good piers for fishing, boat entry, playgrounds, good size sites. A good stop if your in Port Lincoln
Great spot to stay with the kids, cabins were good size and clean. Will definitely be back.

We spent Easter here in the caravan. It was a great place. There was lots to do that didn't involve too much driving. The campsite was great and had a fab camp kitchen and games room. They could do with more washing machines and washing lines as there was a line-up for both.
Great Caravan park with beautiful views across Porters Bay. Direct access to jetty and a beach. Heated swimming pool. Roomy caravan sites. Dog friendly
We had a drive thru van park all elevated and angled so they had bay views

We had an ok stay. The carpark next to the top bitumen sites attracts a bit of antisocial behaviour though like daily burnouts from the P plater coming in for a swim and potential place to park up at night. Had someone's caravan broken into and keys stolen one night while they slept, police the following night driving  around, not sure if something else happened or just patrolling and we actually stopped someone breaking into a neighbours caravan the following afternoon. Toilets are pretty old, with grime and graffiti on door backs, location not too bad. People park in the top next door carpark and come to use the caravan park pool facilities, which seems a bit rich. We didn't have anything stolen or tampered with that we know of but wondering what the point of having coded entry gates is really for if thieves can just park at the top carpark next door (?) it prevents them from actually driving in which is something, but still easy entry into the park

Other than that, we didn't mind the place and can be a decent place to park for a few days to see Port Lincoln.

Large park and we had a level site. Variable shower amenities (some old) but the shower pressure was amazing. Camp kitchen was spacious but the stove was slow and very limited cutlery, cookware. We loved it here and will be back, just need to make sure we have everything!
Recently stayed here for an overnight work trip. The cabin was lovely and very clean. It had everything we needed. Location was convenient. Staff were very accommodating. Will stay again.

Wouldn’t bother staying again.
Started off with a very cold interaction at check in from the receptionist. Didn’t give us much info about the park just tossed us a map.

No powered sites left so we booked unpowered.

Unpowered is a bit off a dust bowl on a pretty steep hill- everyone seemed to struggle to find a flat spot.
Powered looked okay
Amenities are pretty outdated-peeling paint in the showers. Water pressure is good though.
No parents rooms so not great if staying with little ones.
The pier close by is great for fishing

We found the park manager to be a bit bitter after an awkward interaction of them approaching our camp to tell us they’re afraid of dogs? (Mind you it’s a dog friendly camp and we were camping well away from the main areas since we had two young babies)
Our dogs are super friendly and well behaved- we even had other people staying comment on how well behaved our dogs are so it was a bit strange.

Whole experience put us off a bit.
Great location whilst staying in Port Lincoln. Amenities very clean. We had a drive thru powered site. Highly recommend.
We parked our caravan here for an extended stay. Great spot overlooking Boston Bay. WC clean and showers good. Grounds kept well and campers kitchen is excellent. Service is fantastic. I recommend.
Great views and amenities for all types of camping needs.

Stunning ocean front caravan park! Plenty of spots for tents and caravans. Great ocean views, but can get abit windy. Great camp kitchen facilities with a new indoor wash up area and common room with TV's and sitting space. Felt really safe and there was plenty of people around. Reception was friendly and really helpful. Check in was seamless. Nothing to fault. Showers were clean and spacious. You have everything close by, supermarkets, fuel stations etc.

Kleine Stellplätze (Campervan), WC Anlagen dringend renovierungsbedürftig und unsauber, sehr schöne
Aussicht

Great park.... we had two nights and loved it so much we did two more nights on the way home. |Not all sites are level... older bathroom block is in need of upgrading... there is a laundry and camp kitchen and a TV room...and an unheated pool... as far as we could tell every site has an ocean view... there's a fishing jetty and park area for kids & great access to Parnkalla walking trail...we loved it & will be back.
Park infrastructure is old and in need of an update. Good location. Good views across the bay.
Welcoming and friendly hosts great views over Boston Bay and interesting layout.
Powered drive through site. Great views
Had a great caravan site beautiful ocean views
Great park. Great view. Clean amenities. Own jetty to fish from. Swimming beach.
Large caravan park. Most sites were roomy, but some sites were close together. Amenities always clean and the showers were amazing, hot and with great water pressure.

We stayed for one night during the off-season and paid $27 for an unpowered site, which was affordable. However, for a G’day Park, it wasn’t what we expected. We were told to park anywhere across three areas – two on a slope and one near bins and piles of branches, all on sandy dirt. Not ideal, even for one night.
The amenities were old but clean, which was good. We were parked close to one block, which was convenient. We didn’t use the camp kitchen or see the laundries, so we can’t comment on those.
Overall, okay for the off-season price, but we wouldn’t stay longer or recommend it during peak season.
Great spot by the water
Great place with view over the water.
非常好的营地，有完美的厨房和休息室
We all really enjoyed our stay at this caravan park. The cabins are very nice and smell fresh. The caravan spots have an amazing view and are well looked after. I will definitely come back soon. ☺️
Fantastic view, great facilities like huge recreation room, lovely new kitchen and outdoor BBQ. Swimming pool.
Well worth the stop. Beach front and close to some great attractions.
We had a powered sight when visiting in the wet they should have a lot of heavey gravey on the road so you don't slip in the mud
Like this park alot, very neat and tidy park.  Love the outlook over the ocean, not far from town.
Fabulous park on the water, a 10 minute drive from town, Fabulous views if caravanning 👌

Why don't they use the long clothes lines instead of useless Hills Hoist?

We ended up putting up our own clothes line as there was never any space available.😡

This little park in North Shields may be a bit out of town but the view out from awning is beautiful. Lovely clean facilities and the bays are huge. I managed to fit Motorhome car trailer and extend awning on it and still had room. Lovely olive trees make a hedge giving each bay its privacy from neighbours. Take away from roadhouse at entrance was pretty good

Park is right on the beach, big bays and flat, unlike the other park.|Facilities are clean a well maintained with fish cleaning area and jetty 100 m away.|Service station at front of park has great qualty food, chips are the best ever tasted. There is a pub 500m north, easy walk. Could get windy.|Recomend staying when in PL and not the other as it is on the side of a mountain.
Close to the sea, the scenery is great but the wind is very strong. The equipment is old but clean. Note that there is no camp kitchen, only an open-air barbecue area.
We stayed on a powered site ocean front.  Staff very friendly and helpful. Facilities old but very clean, everything worked. Enjoyed our stay.
We had the best spot, unfortunately it was very windy

Great spot
Friendly staff
Clean amenities... close to everything 5 🌟
Great spot and location.. clean and tidy place and well priced I reckon

An ok site, sandwiched between the bay and the main highway.  Luckily we stayed at a weekend, otherwise the road noise would have been loud and intrusive.
Facilities were clean, but dated.
Not really the campsite's fault but watch out for dog shit.

Park ok, nice views, cabins lovely but camping ground (unpowered) very all over the shop and unorganised. Booked un-powered site and very unlevel for van or swags/tents. No allocated area which we were in swags big vans just parking everywhere wherever they could fit this way and that way (including right in front of us cutting off our view of the ocean which annoyed us)

Booked a cabin on the last night which was lovely and owner very accommodating but definitely would not book powered or un-powered site again.

It would be better to have allocated areas for people in tents and swags at the front and vans behind etc.

I was having a great time there until the last morning when the cleaner couldn’t see a car there so she took it upon herself to go and throw out all my food and take my keys back to the office and had nothing to say except “oh dear sorry” Not good enough. Never even offered a meal voucher for all the food they threw away. Reported this to the office and same blank stare and hollow “sorry”.  This was by 9 am in the morning. Not even checkout time.
Well set out caravan park good sized pads,for vans
We stayed for one night and loved this place! The kitchen and bathroom were both very clean.

$40 a night for unpowered site. To paint a picture… it’s a absolutely stunning day I’m on top of a hill under a tree by the water, I haven’t checked out the facilities so can’t comment but I’m sure they are fine, just enjoy.

🌿Probably one of the best caravan parks that we have stayed at in SA. Site 101 .Big sites for the  standard sites. Drive through. Sea views. Huge caravan park with a fabulous pool, rec room, beach . Amenities are a little old but there are new ones too.
Really quiet at night 🌙
highly recommended 👌
.
Nice park. Literally overlooking the jetty and a small sheltered beach. (50 Mtr walk). Neat and tidy facilities. If we’re ever down in Port Lincoln again, we’ll stay here.
One of the best campsite/park we’ve stayed in in Australia. Great location and beautiful views. Will come back again in the future!

Unpowered sites not ideal for the traveler who likes spending their day site seeing. They are unallocated and hard to find a spot when you get there around 4-5pm. Then it’s a long trek to the bathrooms. This is not noted on their website.
Best customer service I have received on my lap of Australia so far. 12 days of paradise with outstanding customer service. I can not speak highly enough of this park. I will be back!

It was ok but overpriced, we stayed in a cabin for 5 nights and the revamp has been done on the cheap. The mattresses are a disgrace and should not be in a commercial accomodation situation, I had backache from day 1 and my wife didn’t sleep properly the whole time. The internet packed up on schedule every 30 minutes or so,  therefore you couldn’t watch the entertainment for a decent amount of time. The pool is way too small for the size of the facility. The fridge and freezer reeked of dead rotten bait, which finally died down after 5 days of applying Glen 20. Every time the fridge was opened the whole room stank for 10 minutes.||That said, the location and view was the redeeming feature but in hindsight I should taken a financial hit and moved to the Port Lincoln hotel which has rooms for the same price, but without the kitchen.

We rented a campsite. It's a very large campsite. We weren't lucky this time. It was very windy and cold. The temperature fluctuated too much. I believe it will be much more beautiful when the sun shines.

Nicely located on a little alcove, it felt safe and inclusive. Amenities were clean, sites were easy to access. Fishing, swimming, pool was great, and a little playground for the kids.
Walks nearby. Nice quiet park.

Three un powered areas all on a lean for people to camp yet not marked bays and  clearly everyone trying to cram in there the way they were basically camped on top of each other due to having no respect for the next camper  not ideal the park needs to do better with that side of things other than that a beautiful spot and close to town for all your shopping needs maybe in off peak season would definitely be better
My favourite caravan park in SA. The views are unbeatable, it’s perfect for families and has all the amenities you need - playground, pool, jetty for fishing and swimming, walks. The best!
Nice cabins clean,great location,needs ceiling fans in rooms
Need to update there playground for kid, very poor

The receptionist was very friendly and helpful. Servo right next door. Jetty just down the road. Clean toilets and showers. You can wash your car and caravan on your site. It's on the water and you can see Port Lincoln across the water. Great place.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Bec
Carley
Rochelle
Bill
AnD CINDY from HOUSEKEEPING
U R A TOP SHELF LADY.. you work so hard they are LUCKY 🍀 to have a worker like you...❣️
if I needed anything, any help with anything. They never hesitated on helping me.
I'm grateful for all the sits I had. AND for letting me stay for way longer than expected..🩷..
THANK YOU
* Sites are very big with some very beautiful Sea views.
* Trees on each side for privacy
- Not Much Glass
- Can be VERY 🌬️💨 WINDY some days
- Awning: i recommend NOT TO put out on those VERY WINDY DAYS..!
* Toilets and Showers are close
* They are Cleaned daily
* Laundry x 2
* Toilets blocks x 2
* Clothes Lines x 6
* This Park is not in port Lincoln it's self. It's only 8 minutes further out of town.
Just outside the fence is a fishing Jetty, BEAUTIFUL PARK for the kids, **⭐ Servo next door that has Hot food made to order, ice cream for hot days and is also the local Post Office, so if you're staying for a little while you can get mail or packages delivered to the Servo..!

Fair enough we knew the park was on the highway. Toilets were clean but old. The grounds needed love and attention. The picture they put on fb shows a pool. There is no pool. None of the above mentioned bothered us but don’t charge people $40. Thirty dollars would have been more reasonable.
Amenities are on the old style side but very clean. Staff are very friendly. We stayed for 3 nights . Prices are great.
Stayed at Powered site with caravan. Shower was the best! Gorgeous beach to have a little swim in. Pool and dog friendly nature of the park was great.

The cabins are clean and comfortable. They may be older but have been very well kept and maintained. The staff are accommodating and friendly.
It isn't in Port Lincoln itself but close enough by car, it has walking distance amenities like the servo, a pub, play ground and The beach is a great beach has a Jetty.
BBQ area in the park is one of the best BBQs I have come across.
Worth the stay, not the Ritz but well worth it. :)

Don’t let the photos fool you!! There is no green lawn!! We had a beachfront site that was not maintained and had bees everywhere on the tree and ground as it was covered in flowers and wasn’t mowed! The dryers don’t work had to put clothes through 4 times still didn’t dry! I asked the front desk for some change she was so rude and said go next door! I called to see how much I needed as I didn’t see the sign and she didn’t answer so I walked back to the office and she was sitting on her mobile looking at fb and ignoring the phone ringing (that was me) |We booked in for a week and lasted 2 nights was a waste of money! The camp kitchen is a bbq doesn’t even have a sink to wash your dishes had to use the laundry sink! The bathrooms ran out of toilet paper and wasn’t filled up til the next day bins overflowing and rubbish flying everywhere because of the wind and not being emptied! Avoid this park
Right on the beach beautiful.  Bathrooms clean and tidy.  Excellent

Fully grassed, spacious well administered caravan park. Facilities little dated but all work extremely well. Maintenance man very proactive and great to talk to.  His local knowledge of the area was exceptional.

The room had a very bad smell to it. The dishes were dirty I had to wash all dishes before use.
The bed had a urine smell to it and very very uncomfortable. Luckily we were only there for afew nights.
I would only recommend this place if you are staying in your own caravan. We stayed in cabin 3.

Stayed a couple of nights & found park nice.
Very windy when we were there which made it impossible to put awning out ,    guess that's what happens when on the coast.  View of Port Lincoln & water was awesome so that made up for it.  Nice big grassed sites & facilities good. Next to hwy but noise not that bad. Ablution block all clean & tidy..10km out of town but would stay again.
Wonderful staff
Room wasn't ready at 2pm..

This caravan park is a gem. It is about 10mins drive from Port Lincoln. You can also ride or walk from the caravan park. The staff are very friendly and helpful. There is a garage at the side of the caravan park and they sell groceries as well. Grassed sites and ocean views from the front sites. A bit windy but bearable. A great place to stay for a while. Price per night  is good. Amenities are great.
Trying call for a booking, phone was lifted off the hook no greeting just left open and then hung up 3 times. If you don’t want business don’t operate as a caravan park!!
Excellent park, fantastic, caring staff great location

A truly wonderful park filled with beautiful staff and management.
After a change in our travel plans, we stayed for 2 months and nothing was ever an issue. If it wasn't Christmas and fully booked, we would still be there. I will miss squidding off the jetty.

The camp kitchen, laundry and amenities block were always in top condition, a credit to both guests and staff. Just wait til you stand on the steps at reception and overlook the water. Just breathtaking!

very nice terraced camping on a steepish slope. most pitches have at least partly sea views.
sizes of pitches do vary. best to ask advice from reception desk.. on 4 dec there were plenty spots still free.
attention for people who have mobility issues.
friendly staff. rubbish bins are always emptied. no smells.
a bit irritating was that every night at 03 and 05 o’clock some of the semi permanent residents drove off to work. this woke me up every night.
Lovely park overlooking the water, place was clean and quiet mostly. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Camp kitchen was very well equipped and kept clean.

Whether you’re staying one night or a week port Lincoln tourist park has what you need in all aspects. It’s even got its very own little jetty perfect for a fish and a little boat ramp perfect for those tinny’s

Highly recommend on your next adventure.

We spent one night in our camper trailer on a powered site. The view was lovely, and the children had a great time on the playground. The camp kitchen is well-equipped, and the dining area is clean and inviting. While the sanitary facilities are a bit dated and somewhat worn, they are clean and functional. The night was quiet until about 3 a.m., when some tradies started their workday and left the campsite in their vehicles. The noise made it impossible for us to enjoy the rest of the night. The campground management should accommodate the residents in a separate area so they aren't disturbed by visitors, and vice versa.

This looked like a promising place but our night was noisy as. Our neighbor had trouble with his caravan and it started making loud sounds at 2.30am. Woke us all, including the kids. Not fun. Next, at 3 am the other neighbor starts his car and lets the engine run for 15mins. From 3am there was constantly cars driving around the campground. There is no quiet time here

We stayed two nights in an off grid camp site. Right on the water with great amenities. TV in main room broken. The unpowered site are large BUT they forgot to mention they are on a slope so very hard to find a level site. I got creative with a big rock on one side to level van. It’s under the Norfolk pine if you want to try. Check in ladies lovely. Windy as on a southerly. Would recommend.
Really easy to deal with, and very clean rooms and awesome views. Has a jetty to fish off so cool

Great views, huge drive through sites. Great amenities. Good walking track runs right across the front of the park. Dog friendly. Areas for dogs to swim. Nice ensuite site, although taps need new washers and could do with better shower heads

My husband and I had the pleasure of staying at Port Lincoln tourist Park for a month in our caravan, we cannot express enough how impressed we were by  management and staff, their professionalism and dedication throughout our entire stay went above and beyond, all staff where warm and friendly.
The View is absolutely breathtaking . The park itself is very well maintained with a wonderful kitchen ,where friends and family can gather around and watch tv or play in the game room.  . The swimming pool is crystal clear and very clean.
The amenities had good hot  water pressure and where clean and tidy..

The atmosphere Within the park was friendly and quiet.
We highly recommend Port Lincoln tourist park for your next Adventure.

Kathleen and David Hill 🙂

We found the caravan park as excellent in every way. We had a drive through site for our caravan and the outlook was to die for. The facilities here are really good and well maintained and there is even a pool and a spa. The camp kitchen was really good and the facilities adjoining it were also new and well maintained. We will come again to this place next time we visit Port Lincoln.
Great location on the beach, with fantastic facilities and close to the town centre.
Great staff.  Top views.  Good location to lincoln.
A great Park in a great location

Great reception staff to accomodate us at the last minute for which we were very thankful. Views were great but we were so disappointed with the ablution block. Very shabby, doors old and water logged at the bottom, especially the disabled women’s shower and missing tiles in the showers, not cleaned very well in the ladies and men’s were even worse. Needs an absolute total revamp of the block we were using. Considering the price charged, we expected a little more.
Great spot and very nice caravan park.

You can’t turn a sow’s ear into a silk purse. I feel sorry for the hard working cleaners at this park. The ablutions are old and decrepit, and no amount of scrubbing can hide broken tiles - on the FLOOR of the shower - deep grime in old tiles, cracked walls, peeling paint.
The new camp kitchen is great. For those sites it faces, which is only half the park. It is miles from most sites, especially the unpowered sites. The rest have to walk right around it to get in, with no paths. In the pouring rain - when you want to use it - it’s slippery and dangerous. Not to mention having to jump over the raging drain, as there is no bridge.
I’d say this chain caravan park group has inherited (bought up) what was a terrific park, and done the bare minimum since. Which has been our experience with this group.
It’s a shame it’s the only caravan park right in Port Lincoln. No competition.
Once again, a disappointing stay.

Stayed a few nights. 2 adults and 1 child. There’s really no faults I can pick, it just wasn’t “outstanding” I guess. By this I mean, it’s basic accomodation. This suited us as we were only there to sleep and shower. Cheap and handy place to stay but maybe bring extra blankets if you feel the cold.
Don’t bother heading to the pub up the road for a beer though (unless you like vinegar).

Great little Caravan Park. Short drive to town. Stayed in winter holidays in a caravan so park was quite. Well looked after. Amenities were fine. There were always empty stalls. Staff were very friendly and happy to help when needed. Sites are very spacious compared to other parks. View of beach was beautiful. Short walk to North Shields Jetty. No luck with fishing this trip. Will definitely be returning.
Love it there, a nice relaxing place, to stay for months
Lovely Scenery and absolutely My best holiday weekend 🥳😁
For what we got it was expensive
Bloody bonza caravan park right on the waterfront. Great views and clean and tidy park.

Great park and heaps of room which is great. Staff friendly and amenities and laundry okay. Short drive to town but amazing scenery.. from Queensland we’d expect 80 km per hour but SA, 110 to town.. love it! 100% recommend and 10 out of 10 from us.

Like stepping back in time, a stay here provides a quiet beachside 1950s style experience. A great find. Check out the home baked goodies next door at the Service station - lovely, and so cheap! Plenty of squid to catch from the nearby jetty.
We had seaside views large drive through site and totally grassy area 😀 👍.

This caravan park was half the price of the other on in Pt Lincoln. The sites are large. The front ones have views of the ocean. The toilets were clean and the girls were friendly.  There is a service station out the front if you need bread and milk.  There is a pizza company  that will deliver.  Pt Lincoln is only 10minutes away. There is a large playground for the kids. The permanents are at the end of the park.

Great park with really generous sized sites, a beautiful outlook and reasonable rates.  The amenities are really clean even though they're a bit older.  All the staff are wonderful - very knowledgeable, friendly and helpful.
Good for short stay
Nice park right on beach
Its just 10-15 mins drive from Port Lincoln center. The cabins are spacious, clean and modern.
Last time I stayed was 40 years ago! It's a different place, the cabins all have an ensuite added, it's impeccably clean & tidy. A great place,well managed, reasonable price, highly recommended.

This is a really good little park and a gem with flat sites, well sized and also  protected. We found it was friendly and quiet as well as managed. There is enough space for everyone and you don’t get crowded out.||I would recommend this to anyone as a base and wanting to explore the region from Tumby Bay, Coffin Bay NP, Cummins and Lincoln NP.||We spent Easter here after 4 days in Coffin Bay … this gave us time to stock up due to the good number of services available in Port Lincoln. My only surprise was how quiet Port Lincoln was with most of the town closed for the 4 days over Easter … sort of a ghost town almost except for your 7 day traders like Coles Woolies Bunnings Liquorland etc … the locals weren’t interested in any tourism dollars eg no bakery was open in the town at all.||The park is on the highway 10km from Port Lincoln and serviced by a garage and takeaway with good coffee available in the morning. The North Shields pub is an easy walk down the road.
Nice and relax place to forget about world
Beautiful caravan park
Great place with water views. Spacious, clean rooms with cooking facilities
Great location,  Immaculate facilities...had a very small site on a slope...smallest of 6 months on the road....as Gday members told not a Gday park despite signs to contrary.
Across road from beach.
Super Campingplatz sehr günstig super Küche und gutes Bad
Very large park.  Efficient and friendly staff.

Friendly staff on the phone helping to accommodate our group last minute.
The showers  and toilets were cleaned daily and the cleaning staff were happy to help. Did a great job

Location was amazing. Kids enjoyed the playground and all it had to offer.
Would recommend again
Thank you 🫶🏼

Port Lincoln TP is a fantastic park to set up and use to explore the area.

They have a variety of Caravan spots including drive through and ensuite sites, dozens of cabins and non powered sites.   👍🏽👍🏽

We've traveled 1700 klms in 2 1/2 months staying somewhere different each night and this is the best place we have stayed in. The facilities are to die for. Awesome rec room, clean and fresh ablution blocks with water pressure to massage you and ample clothes lines. The view is to die for. Out to the water and prime for an amazing sunrise. Highly recommend the staff to.

We stayed on an unpowered site which was fine, had great views. It was pretty windy when we stayed.
Great and clean amenities, we used the camp kitchen that had everything you need, we didn’t get to use the pool but it looked nice.

My partner and I had boat troubles needing repairs quite late at night. Port Lincoln tourist park saved us allowing us to book past the reception time for one of there lovely unpowered sites. They were very accommodating if it wasn’t for their great service we would be in situation opposite to their relaxed and comfortable environment
Good view over the bay, booking/checkin process was easy, staff were friendly, clean facilities, not far from town for supplies.

Great location, beautiful view, clean amenities and easy access to park van.   Staff friendly and helpful.  Our TV had something wrong with it and the office could not have been more helpful in giving us a local number for a repair guy.   Would recommend this park.

We booked in for 4 nights and had the most amazing site looking out over the bay. Ablutions are fantastic with copious and endless hot water. Grass was emerald green and close to town it was the ideal place to explore the bottom of the Eyre Peninsula. Loved it and will be back!

This place is so large it needs its own postcode. Also needs better management. If you make rules then police them. I.e speeding, campfires, loud noise tenant's after 10pm and dog blankets in the washers
Good clean cabin with great view

Good park, amenities are older but very clean. Sites well sized with nice view of the ocean. I watched the most glorious sunrise one morning. Staff were very friendly. Service station out front for milk and bread.
Great place. Walk to pub and small jetty. Ocean views reasonable price.
Staff very welcoming and friendly rooms very clean will stay again
Very good value close to Port Lincoln. Stayed in our own van so no rooms booked. Amenities clean although a bit dated.

Here for 1 night. Had booked 3 nights but got a partial refund for the other 2 nights. So very happy with the understanding of the receptionist.
We stayed in an ensuite family cabin that sleeps 6.
Room was FILFTHY. The surfaces were all tacky and gross. Black hairs on the flooring, in the shower, in the drawers.
Im talking the soles of our feet were black and my babies hands and knees (he is only crawling) were BLACK. We swept, mopped and dusted all surfaces ourselves. The room STINKS of mould? Musty bedding? I cant quite tell but it smells and is intolerable. It literally looks and feels like the room wasnt cleaned at all.

Maybe for a fully set up caravan this place is ideal. Maybe we just got unlucky and got a really bad room. But would never come back here again and really disappointed to say the least.  Really upset we even spent 1 night here and lost our money. What a WASTE
Great park with large sites. Reception is very friendly.

Great little caravan park. Dated but very clean amenities. Great views. Just far enough out of town to be quiet but still close enough to town to drive in to shop etc... loved the sharks mouth entrance!!!

Great caravan park with lush grassy sites! Trees divide all the waterfront sites and are generously sized. There is plenty of squid off the jetty, which isn't far. Would definitely stay here again if we were in the area.
Third time at this park. Amenities are aged but spotlessly clean. Grass sites are very well maintained and the rates are more than reasonable. Would highly recommend 👌

Location is outside of Port Lincoln but very close and nice on the beach. Only accomodation I could find last minute. The park has clean bbq area, and a small playground the kids had a lot of fun at. The cabin is OK, two complaints though 1) the cabinetry is old and so has a damp mould smell, its in the aircon as well. 2) the cabin had strong insense as it wore off after the first day we could smell a strong septic odor coming from the toilet, this became unbearable and hard to sleep, very putrid, I had to spray deodorant to try mask it to stop us going mad. If I had to stay a day longer I would have complained asked for another cabin. For this second point I would recommend making this the last option to stay, I wouldn't come back here. Couldn't see anyone else mention it so maybe we just got unlucky this time. Good luck
Beautiful location. Level site. Nice facilities.
This is a great park heaps off room for your van annex and car, closed to town, right on the ocean, older amenities but very clean, staff very friendly, would highly recommend
Rang the reception number and it went to message. Left a message 3 days ago with no call back. Have heard of others with same issue. Have booked elsewhere.
Great facilities,nice pitch.

Toilet block was a bit outdated.
Very windy 8 day stay.

The staff were nice and the views were amazing and the grounds were clean and organised, The jetty was good, even though it was quite windy, I did manage to catch a squid, but these are the only good things I can say about this park. The amenities were out dated small and cramped,, we asked to be away from other dogs, (as we have an anxious dog) but were placed within 1 site each direction of several other dogs, including one which was not on a lead at anytime during the day. Not much to do around town with anxious dogs.

I’m not sure why this place has 4.3 reviews, but it should be much higher than that! What a beautiful oasis, we will definitely come back😊 Unfortunately we’re only staying overnight but next time we will stay longer.
The amenities were easy to find and very clean. The staff member was very friendly, she had our info ready in the mail box when we arrived with no problems and our caravan view was just magnificent🩵
We recommend this location to anyone looking for a beautiful spot and a pet friendly environment!🥰
Stayed there during Covid in our motorhome looking forward to going back
Fantastic facilities and the view each day is special.Seafood outlets close by and the area is great for day trips

Nice lady on reception. Had a lovely chalet in the park, spacious and comfortable. Nice verandah to sit out on, plenty of bird life to watch. Nice walks, although hilly. Well equipped kitchen and good parking.

A great park. Well located with excellent views over the Bay. Facilities are very clean and well maintained. Large powered sites are easy to access and level.
Staff friendly and efficient.
Such wonderful staff. We decided to extent our stay and they went out of their way to keep us on the same site. The park also offers wonderful view of the bay. We will be back again in the future.
Lovely spot, clean, neat and tidy

Passing thru, so had 2 nights here. Stayed on beach front in our van. Was wonderful. Amenities block very neat and spotlessly clean. Shower pressure almost sent me down the drain. Brilliant.
$35 a night
One of our favourite Caravan Parks. Beautiful views, well kept grounds and facilities.

The best most helpful staff
Can't prase them enough

The perfect place to stay in Port Lincoln. Beautiful water views everywhere you look. A large park which caters for caravans, camper vans, mobile homes, tents, cabins ...

Great facilities, very clean and well maintained. Easy access to town. Fabulous staff to welcome you and provide tourist info.

Plenty of room in the unpowered section, like the ability to be able to choose your spot. I was able to park right at the back near the ocean, no noise and no light so made for quiet a peaceful night.

Guest Kitchen was very modern, has indoor and outdoor section, sitting inside was nice to get out of the howling wind.

Very friendly and supportive staff. We needed a Phillips screwdriver and they were able to help. We had a nice pitch overlooking scrubland and the bay beyond. Showers somewhat dated, but plenty of hot water and clean. The park is very large and is a little way out of town, so you really need a car. Can walk, but bit hilly via road or further via The Parnkalla Track.
Beautiful views over the bay amenities clean and good. Large sites

Had read a few negative reviews online but had a great time here. Had 4 days and check in/check out was easy and staff were very friendly. Facilities were always spotless. Plenty of hot water in showers. We used the covered outdoor kitchen a few times and it has all the facilities required and was always spotless. Free gas stainless steel bbqs were always very clean and ready to use. Had a good powered site with beautiful views of the water.
Great little park on the beach. Can be windy, but not too bad. Had shelter from tbe trees. Nice size sites. Staff were very friendly and helpful.  Would recommend.

This is lovely clean park camp kitchen is very well equipped and  2 tvs, there’s no satellite so you can go and watch whatever in the camp lounge which has very comfortable couch’s ||The sites are wide and beautiful setting right on the water||However |If you are going to allow dogs can you ask the owners to carry a bag and pick there business up,,the lovely walks are great But you spend most of the time looking down to miss the 💩 and there’s a lot |I’ve nothing against dogs but please pick there business up

The cabin my wife and I stayed in was lovely nice and clean with a lovely ensuite. The staff were very friendly. Beautiful views of the waterfront and great fishing. Definitely worth the money. Will stay again in the future. Thx to Rochelle for a warm welcome . Jody and Leigh

We stayed 6 nights ; Great service, Clean amenities, Sites large and the one we had did have good shelter from the windy conditions,
Private and well maintained, Close to a great take away and store next door , easy walk to the jetty and Wheatsheaf hotel , north shields , even had assistance cleaning the squid we caught , Excellence service from Carly , will be back

Beautiful place right on water big caravan site’s amenities older but clean and tidy 10 mins to port Lincoln servo has good food and pizza friendly people sevo and caravan park. Beautiful part of the world.
Lovely place to stay.
Great park very roomy caravan sites and clean amenities

We had an extended stay while waiting for caravan repairs.  Staff were very friendly and accommodating, helping out with extra repairs whilst we were there.  Had a beautiful waterfront site, could see port Lincoln in the distance.  10 min drive there. Amenities were clean and tidy. No worries about staying again. Thanks for all your help.

Stayed here on a powered site in a motorhome, beautiful clean caravan park and amenities block. This park is also pet friendly, which is a bonus. The park is adjacent  to an easy access beach, having  the evening sea breeze blowing through after a busy touring day was lovely. The lady in the office was friendly and helpful when asked the best things to see and do in the area.  Another staff member was spot on when asked where we should go for lunch on Sunday afternoon,  he suggested the Pier Hotel which was awesome.

Caravan site backing on to water was excellent as well as large sites for big rigs
Close to all Port Lincoln has to offer as well
Enjoyed straying here great park
This place is DISGUSTING!!!! I'll sleep in my car on the side of the road before even considering this place again!
Nice spot.
Great location.  Would recommend staying here. Will be back, but for longer next time.
Big park and suitable for large rigs.

We have been here about 3 times in all.  When not wet this is a better park. Sites are private along the beach, which is gorgeous when looking out over the Bay.. there are a few concrete sites.  Facilities are good. Need new washing lines.  Close to town as well.
Great park 👏
This is one of our favourite places to stay. The cabins and facilities are great and the staff are always helpful with tips and suggestions of what to do.

Avoid this park. Sites are too close together, tradies leave noisily almost hourly from 3am, toilets are cleaned at 7am (when people need the toilet). Not the site’s fault, but no TV reception. Very disappointing.

Wow, location, location. One of the better Caravan parks we have been too. Boston Bay Views. This what the mega rich pay for Caravan Style. Uninterrupted views. Concrete level drive thru sites are great. Plenty of room. Early morning watching the Trawler Boat fleet leave the harbour for tuna and king fish. The jetty is great. The rolling green hills in the back ground.

There are no level sites for unpowered that are close to toilets. It’s so hard to get a good spot. We ended up just being at a massive angle otherwise you have to walk ages to use the bathroom.

They clean the toilets at 7-8am ish. Which is exactly when everyone wants to use them. I’ve never known a park to do that. Very inconvenient.

Incredible squid fishing from the jetty. Met loads of people who taught us lots about fishing as newbies.

Good camp kitchen and great seating.

Very disappointed. Paid $45 for an umpowered site which no area of the unpowered was flat, how can a place charge so much to sleep on a massive hill, tents, swags and people with out level equipment are very disadvantaged and paying a top price,  feel like I was robbed, Charged way too much!! The maintenance man mowing and whipper snipping early morning around people's vans and camp site was an absolute joke, people are on holiday's!  Would not recommend staying here!!
Great park. Lots of room. Every site seems to have a great view. Very well maintained. Couldn't ask for more.

Powered site No 4 drive-through. Clean and level. Toilets and showers very clean. Great showers too! Hot and high flow. Kitchen well stocked with appliances and lounge areas really nice. BBQ area excellent. Good walking track nearby and small jetty for fishing.

Came here to stay for 5 days weather was not so kind to us but at least we got one day of beautiful weather views for days up the top of the park we preferred the higher sites as there was way more of a view pricing was reasonable discounted as we are a G'day member.
A wonderfully set out caravan park where you have water views without being blocked by another van. Terraced and drive throughs
Normal caravan park with great views   great ladies in checking.  Busy in cloths washing.  Bit hilly but very usable, not to crowded.  Stayed for five days.  Bring ya fishing rod!

Wonderful park. We had to extend our stay due to a cracked windscreen and Bridgette was very helpful.
The amenities block was lovely, updates, clean and large.
The camp kitchen and lounge is amazing, incredibly well appointed and clean.
The laundry like wise is clean and spacious. There is ample line space as well.
Whilst we didn’t examine every site we were on a very very large drive through site, sadly we couldn’t set up to face the water but we just sat up on the slope behind our van.
Whilst it is a bit of a hike into touwn, the walk on the coastal trail is an easy one and very very pretty.
We look forward to the rest of our stay
Drive through sites well located
Great van park. Helpful staff and lovely views
Ammenities clean , good showers . Lawns get cut regularly. (Nice).

This is a free overnight stay. Great location There a drop for the caravan toilet. There also you can refill the water tank. You need to go to the supermarket buy a token for $2. And you get 60 little. . There a toilets there. And you can see the caravan park just over the tennis court. It a nice walk into town.  About 5 minutes. Go the pub for great food.

Dirty, noisy and staff are unfriendly. The only positive things I can say about this park are that it is cheap and the views are pretty. I stayed in a family cabin for a two-week period. I wasn't provided with hand soap or dishwashing detergent (though I was given four tubes of shower gel). The dishes are greasy, presumably due to the lack of dishwashing detergent. Being right besides the highway, sounds of cars and trucks is expected. What was not expected was for the alarm in the neighbouring petrol station to go off in the middle of the night on three seperate occasions during my two-week stay (I was in cabin 1 which is just the other side of the driveway from the petrol station). Staff are difficult to track down. I arrived just before 5 on a Sunday, roughly 20 minutes after reception had been closed early because the park was quite. I was staying for work reasons so was away from the park during work hours on weekdays. On the weekend, I stopped by reception five times at different times of day to pay my bill and on four of those occasions reception was unmanned. By the way, there is a $50 after-hours call fee, so don't forget your cabin key. My final recommendation is that you bring your own cooktop if stay for more than a night or two, as even though I was told on the phone that cooking facilities are available, those only include an electric frypan (and a pot, despite having no cook top on which to use it).
Power sites overlooking bay, generous size, grassed.  Amenities clean.  Friendly staff.  Entrance to caravan park is by the side of service station.

A fantastic place to stay if you want to visit Port Lincoln and surrounding areas.  Two minutes from the airport. Ten minutes from the center of town . We went for a week and ended up staying two . Did all the National parks as it was better to leave the van there at $27 a night power and water than go to the NPs at upwards of $34 a night (camp site plus car fee ) for a long drop toilet.
i got bit by bed bugs

Stayed at total of 3 nights. Very nice.
The weather wasn't the best, but thems the breaks.
We were treated to a pretty reasonable sunset though.
The park is terraced, offering just about everyone, a nice view of the bay area.
Amenities are as normal, dated, but clean and serviceable.
We took advantage and used the laundry facilities, which were good.
Staff were friendly and attentive.
Great caravan park.  Really good drive through sites.  Very clean ablutions, very clean and tidy park.  Fantastic views across the ocean.

BEST SHOWERS EVER!!! Nowhere else comes close. Location is great. Slopes down to the water/jetty. Plenty of level sites though for caravans and motor homes. Staff friendly. Amenities clean and user friendly.

There is really only one park in town. It's on a very sloped site and if you are in a tent or a roof top tent it's very hard to find a level space. Why they haven't terraced the site I don't know. The check in was pleasant enough. The wifi is useless. This should be fixed also. It's too far to walk into town for food ;-). The views of the water are great. There is a pier to fish from if that's you thing.

Want to give Bridget a 5 star review for her service. Ended up calling the after service number after hours as we felt unsafe at another campsite. She was so helpful, directed us to the campsite which was in an ideal location. Gave us a brilliant spot on the site at 8pm as we were stuck. She knew we’re tired as we did the shark cage diving that day and offered for us to pay in the morning and as someone who is travelling away from home was just caring and mum like! Can’t recommend this place enough and will be telling my friends to stay here. Also must add amenities are spotless and amazing! Thank you Bridget!
Caravan site, are perfect. The view is to die for
Very good location and well maintained. Secured location for our caravan with key access in and out. We visited many spectacular places around Port Lincoln.

The best things going for this park is the location and the cleanliness of the facilities.
The negatives include old, poorly maintained showers & toilets (cleaned as best as possible), and average reception staff.
I acknowledge that the park has had a tough week with vehicles being stolen and vans broken in, however that is not all residents fault.
The grounds were dry, but this was understandable due to the very dry season.
Even though we're members of "Discovery ", I think we'll stay at Port Lincoln Caravan Park next time.
Toilets and showers need a good clean. Hand drier no working. Cleaning ay 7.30 is not a good timing.
Brilliant place and people

Lovely site right next to the ocean! We were on an unpowered site, slightly uneven in places but managed to find a spot that wasn’t too bad, levelling blocks would be recommended. Clean & hot showers, toilets clean too. Stunning views of the sea where you can fish 🎣 friendly receptionists, would recommend!
What a magic place to be for a few days..great views of the bay..clean amenities…helpful and very obliging staff..top marks and would love to come back
Neat and clean accommodations and wonderful staff. 💖

While the amenities to the park were nice and clean and the location was good. The park sites are not good, a lot of the sites listed as drive through are not at all level and it’s impossible to level your van. This is very dangerous and the first site we were in was basically unusable and we asked to move. In our time that we stayed there we saw six more people move from this site. Some of the drive through sites are level but a number of the aparrent drive through sites closer to the water are not and it’s a safety issue.
Espace caravan park fabuleux. Accés, vue mer splendide. Espaces communs propres, fonctionnel et trés bien équipés. Endroit merveilleux.
This is a great caravan park have been here previously and will come back again whe doing the Eyre Peninsula.

Sites were not very even a man had to come help one of our group to unhitch the ground was so slopey.   I was disgusted with the old amenities and we all feel they were disgusting and the worst amenities we’ve stayed in. , They were dirty and the drains were full of hair which could not have happened from one clean to the next. The hooks were all broken so nowhere to hang up your towels, etc.   For the price that you charge you need to invest in updating the older amenities.

A good stop to freshen up after a week in the NP and whalers way. Amenities are clean and fairly new. We only had a 4WD with a RTT and cannot speak much about the larger plot. Easy walk to the small jetty perfect to catch squid
Beautiful Caravan Park with a great view and close to everything
Caravan park

I've stayed at this park for both work stays and leisure several times. I've stayed in a number of different cabins now, from the standard, past the executive, and all the way to the queen executive cabin. As far as the location is concerned, absolutely pearler! It's just on the outskirts of the main part of town and enjoys waterfront views. There are definitely worse views to enjoy your morning cuppa, the park is in an excellent spot with trails on either side of the park to explore with the family. It has its own private jetty for fishing, and its own boat launch! It's only a couple minutes drive from the esplanade and town, making it easy peasy to enjoy the beautiful food and drink the town has to offer.

As far as the cabins are concerned, they are neat, not stinky at all, and present very well. Never had an issue with air conditioning or with heating, and the furniture is fine for the most part. My 4 star review for the rooms is only because the mattresses within the cabins can be a little hit and miss, or so I've found. I've stayed in about 5 different cabins, and two of them had mattresses which affected my back immediately. Most recently, I stayed in a queen cabin and one of the mattresses really affected my upper back and neck. Thankfully the other queen mattress in the cabin was perfect, but if I didn't have a choice that could have been very uncomfortable. However, that is my only gripe; and I can't comment on the quality of all the mattresses in all the cabins. Only from my own experiences. I will say that I do have a bad back though, so I am sensitive to a good or bad mattress. Everything else was fine!!! The standard cabin is fine for a single person. It can sleep up to 6 people, but I wouldn't choose that for a family stay. The executive cabins are far more spacious and would be far more comfortable for 2-6 people. The queen executive had excellent furnishings, a good size smart tv, a very nice and spacious shower, and two decent size bedrooms. My partner and I were very comfortable, particularly after we switched mattresses lol.

The caravan side of the park is big, and is where you'll find the parks recreational features. I haven't really explored that part of the park funnily enough, but I did peer over and see the communal area quite busy one evening with people chatting and enjoying themselves. It looks like a great vibe on that side of the park!

I would definitely recommend this park for your stay. Don't put too much stock into my mattress gripes, my other work mates never complained about them,  excluding one who stayed in the same standard cabin I did one time earlier. I may have just found the bad ones out of bad luck lol. The staff are excellent and accommodating, and have always been helpful to me. I would stay there again!

4.5 out of 5 for my experience.

Nice location.  Good views.  Staff were friendly enough.  |But the old toilet block is a disgrace. |It looks like it never gets cleaned.  It’s old, run down and filthy.  Some shower stalls only have one hook on the door.|It either needs to be replaced or totally renovated.|It makes the $55 p/n a rip off.

Great caravan park, plenty of room on the powered site which are all level and sectioned off.  Three areas for unpowered site and you can park anywhere you like in these but finding a decently level site is almost impossible.  Plenty of amenities around the park, has a couple of little jetties for walking on and fishing from, good little play area for kids, small beach for swimming or setting off in your kayak or canoe.  There is a sign warning about sharks and the fact they do get big sharks in the bay so swim at your own risk. There are walks either end of the park one is a cliff/beach walk the other is into the foreshore in Port Lincoln town itself
Had a powered site right on the waterfront! How lucky were we? Great facilities.... large kitchen, family/TV room , swimming pool,  bike/walking track to town

What a perfect spot to do aslittke or as much as you want. Friendly professional staff greeted on our arrival and we were I believe given one on the nicest site to park for our stay for the week.
Spotless amenities,  new infrastructure and views to die for.
Thank you for an awesome first time visit to Port Lincoln, will definitely recommend and come back again.
Nicely laid out park, plenty of space between sites. Lovely location with a good gravel walk trail that is right along the waters edge.
Great park, very spacious, clean amenities and reasonably priced
Nice place clean showers and toilets great camp kitchen not great that locals kids r known for coming in at night to take stuff
Great park, big lots of sites and choices. Has a pool and playground and the amenities were clean and accessible. Decent camp kitchen and rec room.

This was a well laid out caravan park. We had a drive through site which offered plenty of space.
The facilities were clean and the grounds are well maintained.
If you like fishing then bring your rods as they have their own private jetty.
Dog friendly, with dog friendly beaches and eateries.
Close to town centre which has everything you need.
Walking tracks for exercise.
It’s great for all ages.
Fantastic park, big sites, beach access, great shallow spot for a dip. Wanted more days but couldn't keep our spot. Very busy park.

Booked standard site 69 Did this via a phone call to the park many months out: was 2/3rd the size of any CP we have ever been to + it had a tree which is not shown on their park map (other trees are shown), none of this information was conveyed to me when booking & this site was actually suggested by reception when the other sites I requested were already booked, We could only place the caravan with awning side facing up the hill directly at the caravan opposite & could not open the awning fully (max. halfway) as it would have protruded out into the roadway - still this was better than the ridiculous / unproffessional situation with unpowered sites: the CP is over priced & it under delivered - will never be back

What a wonderful caravan park. So peaceful and all the neighbours were friendly and respectful of each other. We stayed at an ensuite site with a view of the ocean and local jetty. Kids had a ball, staff were friendly and helpful. Great location to be based for exploring all around Port Lincoln and adjoining towns. Amenities and facilities were clean and tidy. We will definitely be back.
Great view, good spot to stay. We didn’t use the facilities and had an unpowered site but it was a large park with lots of options.

I can not fault the fantastic customer service we have received from the staff at the Port Lincoln Tourist Park.
After emails & calls were followed up to get a site secured & booked near a group we were going to be travelling with.. To then unfortunately having to cancel due to ill health.
The staff were kind & understanding to our situation. And unexpected refund of the initial booking cost.
Above & beyond custom service that can be rare these days. Will not hesitate in recommending this Park to our network.

I stayed here for one night on an unpowered site with my campervan but was a bit disappointed from the price-performance ratio.

The unpowered sites all across the park are very steep dirt patches where it’s basically impossible to find a reasonable flat area if you don’t own some wedges you can chuck under your tires.

Also the Wifi was almost not existent which is unfortunately a general thing all across Australia. It seems they only provide as much Wifi as necessary so promote they are providing some at all.

I was really satisfied with the amenities and especially the camp kitchen on the other hand. The amenities are not very modern but they are clean and everything works fine.

The kitchen is very spacious and you got all kinds of stuff like toasters and kettles.
Also the seating area was really new and clean.

To come to a conclusion, I would say it’s a very good place if you don’t mind sleeping at an angle or if you have some wedges.
All the powered sites are perfectly level but out of my price range.

Otherwise I had a good time here and the staff was really friendly as well.

Great location overlooking Boston Bay, nice waterfront walk to town, small jetty to fish from. We are all self contained so we had a unpowered site but there are a few water taps scattered around so you can fill up or even connect if no one is nearby.

We arrived with our motorhome and booked an ensuite site. Spectacular location! Fantastic and friendly staff .Amenities cleans , big clean kitchens and very nice living room with comfortable sofas , good simming pool for little kids too,  area and sites clean and  quiet place ( though huge place) . 4 unforgatable stay .
Great park. Good pool. Really good camp kitchen.

Lovely view, large sites. My suggestion would be to have an allocated washing machine for dog washing linen. My clothes dried smelling like a wet dog. Being allergic to dogs I had to re-wash as I was getting asthma you could smell doggy in our van. I love dogs and understand wanting wash their towels extra but waisted a whole day re-washing trying to get rid of smell and obviously dog hair. On a positive note to finish, the seafood basket was yummy. Will be back just won't do washing.
All the unpower site are without site number and uneven. Not worth the money. Also, someone stayed in the camp kitchen very late at night, talking loudly and making big noise.
Lovely staff for bookings, easy to find and Lovely location.

Massive fail this park was - considering its prime location this park lacks everything. There is NO grass - its all dead and a dustbowl, id hate to be here during a rain. Got no idea what the ground staff do but defs not watering. The park facilities are all old and outdated except for a new swimming pool - which is so small and doesnt meet the size of the park. There is nothing for the kids to do, the roads are either dirt or lose gravel so dont bring the scooters bikes only! There is a newish kitchen gamesroom hub which has nothing but stained couches and big tv, and 1 game machine with pac man lol... this is the first park our ensuite site came with no towells at all, staff were rude when queried this. Everything was such a letdown with this park which im suprised gday parks is affiliated with.
Clean facilities that were never crowded. A staff member stopped me from drinking boiled vinegar. There's places to fish without leaving the site.

We booked 6 nights on an unpowered site. However there is just a steeply sloping paddock for the unpowered bookings. There are no marked out sites, its a total free for all, park wherever you like, with people blocking other people in or parking right in front of other campers. We moved 3 times in 6 nights. It was around New Year and busy but one camper decided to play his idea of good music over a tannoy till after 1 am on 2 nights. Management seems to be hands off. The water mains are exposed above ground so that the water heats up on sunny days. There is a deep manhole in the centre with a lose metal cover on it. We watched numerous kids lifting the cover off, none fell down the hole but one kid cut his foot on the metal sheet covering it. It wouldn't be strong enough to support a vehicle if driven over. The unpowered area has no kitchen or hot water sink and a basic laundry with 3 machines. You have to walk over to the powered area where there are only 3 sinks for the whole campsite.

Lovely park but really big and busy now. Perfect location but you need to book in really early if you want to be close with friends and family. Amenities were great but being on a hill, find it hard for the kids to ride their bikes or scooters.

What an incredible stay! We are currently doing a lap of Australia and needed somewhere to stay after an injury, the manager was so incredibly friendly and we ended up spending an extra night. The amenities and grounds were some of the cleanest we’ve come across! The toilet block seemed to be cleaned multiple times in the day. The unpowered camping was right on the water and walking trail with frequent pelicans. What an incredible place!
Bets hike ever...

Great Caravan Park.  Everything about it is good!!
Great walk to town, very well designed park, staff are amazing!!
Recommend

Menos el wifi todo excelente
Las zonas comunes, la atención y la limpieza todo perfecto. La vista obvio increíble!

Pre booked 2 night stay. Powered site, 2 adults October. Very hilly park, some have been levelled for drive through. Some properly terraced, others are questionable. Park management need to get a bobcat or similar and get these sites level, smooth access and have safety at the forefront.

Five stars for the location. The sites are good, although on our site (with a slab), we could not get the van level. I was less than impressed that I had to go from toilet to toilet to find one with toilet paper. With the Ladies toilet blocked off at one of the amenities blocks, this one is servicing the whole van park and to find that half the toilets do not have toilet paper is just not good enough.this was at 9pm. Then this morning, my husband went to the toilet around 8 am only to find it being cleaned - at a time when people are often following the call of nature and/or preparing to leave the park. All other parks that we have used leave the cleaning until around 10:30 am after checkout time. My husband went to the other toilet block (the one that has blocked off the female toilet) to find the only toilet not occupied was blocked up with toilet paper.
The issues were raised with administration staff, which is why toilet paper was available in the Ladies the following night. My husband also made a complain about the Gents toilet following his experience.
It says enough that it took 2 months for a response to this review and the response was pretty bland, indicating a poor quality control process.

This caravan park can only be described as 'fine'

The location is 'fine'
The sites are 'fine'
The view is actually pretty decent, but that's where it ends.

In the 2 days we've been here (midweek, half our stay), the amenities have yet to be cleaned, and judging by the state of them, they haven't had a decent clean in years.

There is a literal lake of piss in the male toilets due to a plumbing issue.
The stalls are falling apart and a decent number don't have locks or latches.

It's almost like the additional amenity blocks are locked up most of the year, waiting for peak times, only to be opened with the bare minimum of effort (unlock the doors and turn on the lights), but this doesn't explain the main or 'newer' of the 3 amenity blocks being in the same condition.

The sites are a bit pokey in places due mainly to the terrain, which makes levelling vans difficult, with one van we saw requiring literal rocks under it to get it level.

There is a reasonably new pool, but with all things considered, I can't help but feel this money could have been better spent on some basic maintenance of the core facilities, with swimming duties taken care of by the 'beach'

All in all, I couldn't recommend anyone actually books this park without a fully self contained van (one where you know it will be cleaned) when there is plenty of campgrounds in the surrounding national parks where you could have a shower standing in the dirt.
